name: ReactMCP — React Application MCP Server
what_it_does: ReactMCP server enabling AI agents to interact with React applications — querying component trees, generating React components, managing component state inspection, and integrating React application development into agent-driven frontend development and UI automation workflows.
use_cases: Generating React components from specification from UI development agents
Inspecting component hierarchy and props from debugging agents
Automating React component creation from design-to-code agents
Testing React component behavior from QA agents
Documenting component interfaces from documentation agents
Building React component libraries from design system agents
not_for: Backend or API development (this is frontend/UI focused)
Production React app manipulation at runtime (development tool)
best_when: A frontend developer working with React needs AI agents to generate components, inspect application structure, or automate UI development tasks.
avoid_when: You don't use React, or need full-stack automation — use more general code generation MCPs.

auth.notes: No authentication — local React development tool. May integrate with React DevTools or local development server.

known_agent_gotchas: React version compatibility — React 18 and 19 have different component patterns
Local development server must be running for runtime component inspection
TypeScript vs JavaScript React projects may need different configuration
Component generation quality depends on how well the agent understands your design system
Community MCP — limited testing across diverse React configurations
Generated components may not follow project-specific conventions without context
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local React development tool. No credentials. No external network access. Community MCP. Generated code should be reviewed before committing.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local development tool. No credentials. Community MCP. Review generated code.
